verdict.answer_first: B starts with the usable definition: "Big-O describes how an algorithm's cost grows"; A opens with a title then the definition, so B gets to the point faster.
verdict.decisiveness: B ends with one practical prescription: "nested loop...reach for a hash map"; A gives two fixes plus a follow-up menu, "specific case...database queries / pandas / a particular language," which weakens the landing.
verdict.fabrication: A has the more unsupported claim: "cover the majority"; B also says "single most common," but A adds a broader quantified-sounding claim without evidence.
verdict.actionability: A has stronger runnable detail and concrete scale math: "10,000 users → 100,000,000" and the hidden `item not in result` trap; B is easier to consume but less diagnostic.
